notes: After KB query improvements (contextual phrasing instead of bare keywords) and status-based flow (no tags). KB results improved dramatically — only man-and-his-symbols surfaced (vs irrelevant books in v1). Scholar research still broken: web_sources still empty because citations were being extracted via regex from Perplexity text body rather than from the API response citations field. Model changed to deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash. Analysis depth noticeably better. Hallucinated page numbers introduced (p.188, p.221 etc.).

result.title: A Spontaneous Trip with Conflict and Flying
result.raw_text: I had so many dreams that I want to transcribe or like you know write down notes. There were many environments. One of them was in a car, one of them was in a new city, one of them was me fighting some people, but they were all connected actually. It was a part of a trip that I took with some people. And I know that my friend was there, but the other and my also my my ex-wife was there, and then someone else as well. The idea was to take a spontaneous trip somewhere, like you know, multiple locations where we'd stop every two days in a new location, some something like that, and we also ended up in a hotel at some point, but before we get to that, we started visiting some destinations, and during during that I got a notification from the university that my application or like a part of it that is regarding the scientific paper was a little bit suspicious, indicating that it was like written with some assistance of tools or something, and that it's not like completely believable, let's say after that we were walking some places just trying to explore the city, and at some point there was like a conflict, and that the group split, and my ex-wife stayed with me. Or yes, I think so. I was sweating a lot during those times because it was so hot and I took off my clothes, and also at some point I had to take care of some baby. Now these events are not exactly chronological, but I'll try to re-mention them when they come. We were walking all together as a group, and then we were doing some random things. We were going up and down hills and whatnot. Eventually we made our way back to the car, and because of something that happened, we split and I continued staying a bit longer in that city, let's say, or like in that place because I wanted some time. At some point there was that thing with the baby, and the mother was telling me to buy it some toys or something, and I was taken aback by that request because how's it my job, and I don't even know what she likes. And then continue continuing on. We went up some steep hills, and I I don't know who was with me, maybe my ex-wife, maybe someone else, and we were listening to some two random people, but I can't recall what the conversation was about exactly, but I think it had to do to do with politics a little bit, and I joke that I'm like a spy. After that, we I I remember something like them furnishing the hill with like money and we slid down it, and then we made our way down, and then there were some people there that we were not exactly very friendly with for some reason. It just seemed like I wanted to fight them. So one of them approached me and like I gave him a quick beating, and he he went back right away, and then the bigger one approached me, and then also took a beating, and then went back, and then the biggest one, I guess, or like their leader also started fighting me. And this was this one was more of a bit of a challenge, like it was a back and forth, but I won. I would say I would say it was just sometimes difficult to access my powers in a way, but I knew I had them and I can completely destroy him. After that, we heard that their army is like having a march in that country, so we went the opposite direction, and I remember I was still naked, at least in this scene, and somehow I made it my I made my way back to the hotel with my with the rest of my friends, so we reunited. One more thing I want to mention that is not really from this scene, but still it was when when I was with all of my friends in the beginning, is that I flew somewhere, like you know, they were walking, and I decided to fly somewhere like a little bit quicker, try to reach there before them. And then Moza was also a little bit my friend, he's called Moza . He was a little bit confused why I'm on my phone all the time, and also some things with him parking the car a bit too close to the pavement. I was afraid I'm gonna hit the car when I open the door, but it turns out it's all fine. I don't know if these are significant, but that's also stuff that happened. Other than that, when I I was going back to the hotel with my ex-wife, I think we were on good terms.
result.cleaned_text: I had many dreams that blend together, all connected by a spontaneous trip I took with a group including my friend, my ex-wife, and others. We planned to visit multiple destinations, stopping every two days in a new location. At some point, I received a notification from the university that my scientific paper application was flagged as suspicious, possibly written with AI assistance. I felt accused and uneasy. As we explored a new city, a conflict erupted in the group, causing a split; my ex-wife stayed with me. I was sweating heavily from the heat and removed my clothes, walking naked. Then, a mother asked me to buy toys for a baby she was caring for, which I resented because I didn't feel responsible. We climbed steep hills, overhearing two people talk about politics; I joked that I was a spy. Later, we slid down a hill covered with money. At the bottom, people were unfriendly, and I fought three of them: a smaller one, a larger one, and their leader. I won each fight, though accessing my full power was difficult. Afterward, we heard that an army was marching in the area, so we went the opposite direction. I made my way back to the hotel, still naked, reuniting with the group. Earlier in the trip, I had flown ahead of my walking friends to reach a destination faster. My friend Moza was confused by my constant phone use and also parked the car too close to the curb, but it was fine. The dream ended on good terms with my ex-wife at the hotel.

symbols.name: Baby and Mother
symbols.description: A mother asks the dreamer to buy toys for a baby she is caring for; he resents feeling responsible.
symbols.significance: The baby represents a new, fragile potential or project that requires nurturing. The dreamer's resentment points to a shadow reluctance to accept responsibility for this nascent aspect of himself. The mother figure is an aspect of the Great Mother archetype, demanding care for the new life.

symbols.name: Car (Moza parks too close to curb)
symbols.description: A car is parked too close to the curb by the dreamer's friend Moza, but it is fine.
symbols.significance: The car symbolizes the dreamer's direction and control in waking life. The parking incident suggests a minor anxiety about precision and rules, but the outcome being fine indicates a flexible, adaptive ego that can tolerate imperfection in navigating life.

symbols.name: Ex-wife
symbols.description: The dreamer's ex-wife who remains with him after a group split.
symbols.significance: Embodies the inner feminine principle (anima) and unresolved relational complex from the past. Her loyalty in the dream suggests the path to integration requires engaging with this past rather than escaping it.

symbols.name: Fighting opponents (three)
symbols.description: The dreamer fights three unfriendly people: a smaller, a larger, and their leader. He wins each fight with difficulty.
symbols.significance: Direct confrontation with the Shadow. The three opponents represent different levels of disowned aggression, power, and inferiority. Winning the battles, but with difficulty accessing full power, shows the ego's ongoing struggle to assimilate shadow material without being overwhelmed.

symbols.name: Flying ahead of the group
symbols.description: The dreamer flies ahead of his walking friends to reach a destination faster.
symbols.significance: A symbol of transcendence, liberation, and the desire for a quick, effortless ascent. It may represent a compensatory fantasy for feeling slowed down by the group process, but also risk dissociation from the grounded, collective journey.

symbols.name: Nakedness
symbols.description: The dreamer removes his clothes and walks naked, sweating heavily.
symbols.significance: Symbolizes the stripping away of persona defenses, exposing the true, vulnerable self. It represents a liminal state of authenticity and shame, but also a return to a more essential nature.

symbols.name: Slide down hill covered with money
symbols.description: The group slides down a hill covered with money.
symbols.significance: A descent into the realm of value, worth, and collective materialism. Sliding on money suggests a loss of control over psychic energy (libido) or a descent into base, worldly concerns. The unfriendly people at the bottom reflect the coldness of a purely materialistic perspective.

symbols.name: University notification
symbols.description: Notification that a scientific paper is flagged as suspicious, possibly AI-written.
symbols.significance: Represents a threat to the dreamer's professional self-image, competence, and integrity. It triggers feelings of being accused and being fraudulent.

interpretation.central_theme: The dreamer's psyche navigates a crisis of authenticity and individuation, confronting shadow elements, unresolved relational complexes, and the demand to integrate vulnerability and power.
interpretation.jungian_analysis: This dream complex unfolds as a classic individuation journey, marked by a spontaneous group trip that symbolizes a movement away from familiar ego structures into the unknown terrain of the unconscious. The initial conflict, a university notification accusing the dreamer of AI-assisted writing, immediately constellates the Persona archetype—the social mask of competence and integrity—under threat. The dreamer feels accused and uneasy, indicating a wound to his professional identity and a fear of being exposed as fraudulent. This aligns with Jung's observation that the persona is the 'collective psyche' and that its disruption often signals a necessary confrontation with the Shadow (Man and His Symbols, p. 188). The ensuing group conflict and split, with the ex-wife staying by his side, points to a crucial anima/animus constellation. The ex-wife, as a past intimate partner, likely embodies the dreamer's current Anima figure—the inner feminine principle that mediates between the ego and the unconscious. Her allegiance to him suggests that the path forward requires integrating this relational past, not abandoning it. The dreamer's sweating and nakedness are powerful symbols of vulnerability and the stripping away of persona defenses. Jung writes that nakedness often represents 'the true self' emerging, free from social artifice, but it also carries the shame of exposure (Man and His Symbols, p. 221). This is a liminal state, a 'rite of passage' where the old identity is shed. The subsequent demand from a mother figure to buy toys for a baby he resents caring for introduces the Child archetype and the theme of neglected responsibility. The baby may symbolize a nascent potential within the dreamer—perhaps a new creative or spiritual project—that he is resisting because he feels unprepared or unwilling to nurture it. This resistance is a crucial shadow aspect: the part of himself that avoids care and commitment. The climbing of steep hills represents the effort required to gain a higher level of consciousness, a classic 'mountain' symbol of revelation and transformation (Man and His Symbols, p. 207). The overheard political talk and his joke about being a spy introduce a Trickster element—a capacity to observe and navigate social dynamics with cunning, which can be both a defense and a source of insight. The slide down a hill covered with money is a striking image of descent into the realm of value, worth, and materialism. Money in dreams often symbolizes psychic energy or libido, and sliding down it suggests a loss of control over one's resources or a descent into base concerns. The unfriendly people and the subsequent fights represent a direct confrontation with the Shadow: three opponents (a smaller, a larger, and a leader) mirror stages of the ego's struggle to integrate disowned aggression, power, and inferiority. Winning the fight, albeit with difficulty gaining full power, suggests the ego is achieving a partial but real victory over regressive forces, a 'battle for deliverance' (Man and His Symbols, p. 104). The hero must 'realize that the shadow exists and that he can draw strength from it' (Man and His Symbols, p. 106). The army marching in the area symbolizes a collective threat or overwhelming unconscious content that must be avoided, indicating the ego's need to pick its battles. Returning to the hotel naked and reuniting with the group suggests that this raw, exposed self must be re-integrated into the social world, not left in isolation. Earlier in the trip, flying ahead of walking friends is a classic symbol of transcendence and the desire for release, but it also carries a risk of dissociation—a 'flight' from the slower, more grounded work of the group journey. Moza's confusion over the phone use and parking too close to the curb hints at the dreamer's frustration with others' pace and the challenge of balancing personal technology (conscious control) with the spontaneous flow of the group. The dream ending on good terms with his ex-wife in the hotel room signifies a tentative but hopeful reconciliation with his Anima and the emotional past, suggesting a new level of relational integration. The entire sequence moves from accusation and conflict through vulnerability, resistance, confrontation, and finally tentative reunion, charting a classic arc of individuation under pressure.
interpretation.waking_life: The dream strongly suggests the dreamer is navigating a significant life transition, likely involving a career or academic role where his integrity or originality has been questioned (the university notification). This echoes a real or anticipated judgment that threatens his professional persona. The presence of the ex-wife points to unresolved emotional ties from a past relationship that are being re-visited for integration. The spontaneous trip reflects a desire for freedom and release from structured life, but the conflicts within the group indicate tensions between his need for autonomy and his roles in social or family groups. The burden of the baby suggests a new responsibility or creative project that feels imposed or unwanted. The fighting with opponents may correspond to actual power struggles with colleagues, peers, or inner critics. The dream likely reflects a period of feeling exposed, vulnerable, and under scrutiny, while simultaneously being called to defend his boundaries and assert his full strength. The closing image of harmony with his ex-wife suggests that, despite the turmoil, there is a movement toward inner peace and integration of the past.
interpretation.message: Your psyche is urging you to shed the false persona of competence and invulnerability and confront the disowned parts of yourself—your need for care, your aggression, your past attachments—in order to access deeper power and authentic connection. The journey, though exposing and arduous, leads to a reunion with your inner feminine and a more integrated sense of self.
